content: Resistant hypertension: BP above goal despite concurrent use of 3 antihypertensive agents of different classes (including a diuretic) at optimal doses, or controlled BP requiring ≥4 medications. Evaluate for medication nonadherence, white coat effect, lifestyle factors (high sodium intake, obesity, alcohol), and secondary causes. Most common secondary causes: primary aldosteronism (5-10% of resistant HTN), obstructive sleep apnea, renal parenchymal disease, renal artery stenosis. Screening for primary aldosteronism: aldosterone-to-renin ratio. Renal artery stenosis suspected with flash pulmonary edema, asymmetric kidneys, rise in creatinine >30% after ACEi/ARB initiation. Spironolactone is most effective fourth-line agent for resistant hypertension (PATHWAY-2 trial). Consider referral to hypertension specialist.
